statement.markdown:
Given a 9x9 Sudoku grid represented as a 2D array, write a function to determine if it is a valid Sudoku configuration. The function should return True if the grid is valid, and False otherwise. The Sudoku grid should have the following properties: \ 
       Each row should contain the numbers 1-9 without repetition. \ 
       Each column should contain the numbers 1-9 without repetition. \ 
       Each of the nine 3x3 sub-grids of the grid should contain the numbers 1-9 without repetition. \ 
       The grid should be a 9x9 2D array.
statement.inputFormat: The function will take a 9x9 2D array as input, where 0 represents an empty cell.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a boolean value indicating whether the grid is valid or not.
statement.notes: Note: The input grid is guaranteed to have no duplicate numbers in the same row, column, or sub-grid, except for empty cells.

examples.explanation: This grid is a valid Sudoku configuration.

starterCode.python: "class Solution: def is_valid_sudoku(self, grid: List[List[int]]) -> bool: return True"

buggyStarterCode.cpp: "#include <vector> class Solution {public: bool isValidSudoku(vector<vector<int>>& grid) { for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < 9; j++) { if (grid[i][j] == 0 && (i < 3 || j < 3)) continue; } } return true; } };"
buggyStarterCode.java: "public class Solution {public boolean isValidSudoku(int[][] grid) { for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < 9; j++) { if (grid[i][j] == 0 && (i < 3 || j < 3)) continue; } } return true; } }"
buggyStarterCode.python: "class Solution: def is_valid_sudoku(self, grid: List[List[int]]) -> bool: for i in range(9): for j in range(9): if grid[i][j] == 0 and (i < 3 or j < 3): continue return True"
buggyStarterCode.javascript: "function isValidSudoku(grid) { for (var i = 0; i < 9; i++) { for (var j = 0; j < 9; j++) { if (grid[i][j] == 0 && (i < 3 || j < 3)) continue; } } return true; }"

solutions.python: "class Solution: def is_valid_sudoku(self, grid: List[List[int]]) -> bool: for i in range(9): for j in range(9): if grid[i][j] == 0: # empty cell if i < 3 or j < 3 or (i >= 3 and i < 6) or (j >= 3 and j < 6) or (i >= 6 and i < 9) or (j >= 6 and j < 9): return False # invalid position # check row if i in [0, 3, 6] and 0 <= j-3 <= 2: num_set = set() for k in range(3): num_set.add(grid[i][j-3+k]) if len(num_set) != 3: return False # check column if j in [0, 3, 6] and 0 <= i-3 <= 2: num_set = set() for k in range(3): num_set.add(grid[i-3+k][j]) if len(num_set) != 3: return False # check 3x3 box if (i // 3) * 3 + j // 3 in [0, 1, 2]: num_set = set() for x in range(3): for y in range(3): num_set.add(grid[(i // 3) * 3 + x][(j // 3) * 3 + y]) if len(num_set) != 9: return False return True"
solutions.cpp: "#include <vector> class Solution {public: bool isValidSudoku(vector<vector<int>>& grid) { for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < 9; j++) { if (grid[i][j] == 0) { // empty cell int block_i = i / 3; int block_j = j / 3; if ((block_i == 0 && block_j == 0) || (block_i == 2 && block_j == 2)) return false; // invalid position if (block_i == 0 && block_j == 1) || (block_i == 0 && block_j == 2) || (block_i == 1 && block_j == 0) || (block_i == 1 && block_j == 2) || (block_i == 2 && block_j == 0) || (block_i == 2 && block_j == 1)) { // check row num_set = std::set<int>(); for (int k = 0; k < 3; k++) num_set.insert(grid[i][j-3+k]); if (num_set.size() != 3) return false; } else if ((block_i == 0 && block_j == 0) || (block_i == 1 && block_j == 0) || (block_i == 1 && block_j == 1)) { // check column num_set = std::set<int>(); for (int k = 0; k < 3; k++) num_set.insert(grid[i-3+k][j]); if (num_set.size() != 3) return false; } else { // check 3x3 box num_set = std::set<int>(); for (int x = 0; x < 3; x++) for (int y = 0; y < 3; y++) num_set.insert(grid[(i // 3) * 3 + x][(j // 3) * 3 + y]); if (num_set.size() != 9) return false; } } return true; } } }"
